summary: First-party seller consent path: seller landing pages create consent records (the clean, lawful contact route), and a read-only intake queue + promotion workflow turn those records into operator work without mixing them into scraped/skip-traced contacts.

files.role: makeConsentRecord() / consentToContactCandidate(): validate and shape first-party consent.
buildSellerIntakeQueue(): pure read model over consent records -> daily intake queue, preserving allowed channels.
buildSellerPromotionWorkflow(): links consented sellers to matching properties/proof + allowed/blocked actions.
Seller price evidence extracted/stored from intake context.
complianceCheck() applied to consent-derived contact candidates.

routes.summary: Records a first-party consent record (lawful basis); can flip outreach_allowed:true on consented channels without paid skip-trace.
Promotion workflow linking consent records to properties (buildSellerPromotionWorkflow).
Extracts seller price evidence from records.

tests.role: Compliance gate tests on consent-derived candidates.

tables.name: consent_records
tables.role: First-party consent leads (name/phone/email/address/channels/source/offer/legal_basis). Created lazily by the seller-lead/seller-intake routes.

tables.name: seller_price_evidence
tables.role: Seller price evidence joined into intake/promotion.

tables.name: properties
tables.role: Properties the promotion workflow matches consented sellers against.

docs.role: Defines first-party consent as the clean seller contact route.

audit_findings.finding: Seller Intake view has a first-party consent path, but consent should become an inspectable graph edge: consent channel, timestamp, matching property/proof, allowed actions, blocked actions.
First-party consent is the compliance-clean contact basis and must stay separate from scraped/skip-traced contacts.

next_actions.action: Promote consent into an explicit graph edge (channel + timestamp + legal_basis) that operators can inspect and route around.
Show allowed vs blocked actions per consented seller directly in the intake queue.
